title: Monday in the Gmail Sidebar / Google Enterprise & Monday for Chrome
excerpt:
Monday and Google - better together 
I love Monday, but I’m missing it within Gmail and believe a lot of value is being left on the table without the integration. Essentially, many of the apps I use and pay for are baked…

posts.author: LambertStrategy (Avi Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday and Google - better together

I love Monday, but I’m missing it within Gmail and believe a lot of value is being left on the table without the integration. Essentially, many of the apps I use and pay for are baked into Google Enterprise, such as DocuSign, Evernote, MixMax etc.

Gmail, Chrome and the Gsuite Marketplace

While I can trigger automations within Monday that take data from Gmail and place it into my boards as pulses, I would love the ability to create actions from within Gmail. A Chrome plugin could do this with the Gmail sidebar SDK. Or, Monday could be listed from with the GSuite Admin.

In either case, for a Gmail Add-on, Chrome Extension, or App in the GSuite marketplace I’m very surprised Monday is not whitelisted there as a project management app. Other competitors like Asana, Mavenlink and Trello are there in the GSuite Marketplace.

Getting Down to Brass Tacks

I use Gmail for sales and business, as well as managing my company. So, I think Monday for Gmail would be an easy win. Moreover, Monday for Chrome would be smart also, another slightly different but similar feature request.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! Clickup does this well with their Chrome extension. Is love to see something similar for Monday.

https://docs.clickup.com/en/articles/1781778-chrome-extension-time-tracking-create-tasks-bookmark-sites-add-notes-screen-captures-and-capture-emails?_ga=2.213395351.2080161678.1569900393-227882785.1568368933

posts.author: LambertStrategy (Avi Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here’s an example of what I’m talking about, within the Gmail sidebar. Monday would be excellent in the Gsuite as a happy customer. I have nothing but when I don’t see it here, with all the ratings and review left on the table.

Screenshot 2019-10-07 at 16.29.37.png2670×1758 259 KB

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also love to see a Chrome Extension. I use the OneNote and Fireshot Extensions regularly. It would be great to have a Monday Extension that sends webpages to items, and/or could screenshot sites and save that to the files column of a new item.

posts.author: LambertStrategy (Avi Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks CWIAassoc, that’s precisely what I was looking for, to have Monday on Chrome, and inside the Google Suite marketplace.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @LambertStrategy @cwiassoc @TheOtherPM! Great news coming your way soon - we are working on a Chrome extension for you to add items to your board. it is currently in the alpha phase but know that we’re looking to get this out to beta in the coming months if not sooner! Stay tuned for this development! Cheers!

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thanks for your interest in this capability I wanted to share a quick update on this.

As our team’s priorities have shifted with the current global situation, the timeline for the Chrome extension has been pushed back. We’re expecting to start working on this again towards the end of next quarter. We will be sure to update you as we have more information!

posts.author: LambertStrategy (Avi Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @melissav-monday.com

Thank you kindly for the detailed update on when the Google Enterprise will be started.

posts.author: OfficeHounds (Maggie Langley)
posts.cookedHtml:
@lauralev Is there any news on this?

I would very much like a Clickup style Gmail addon that allows us to add an email as a comment to a Monday project right from Gmail. I feel this is the one thing that you are really missing!

posts.author: Nicholas (Nicholas Hennell-Foley)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t wait for this feature! Let us know if you need any testing done @melissav-monday.com

posts.author: Mannetjie (Klaas J Van Rooyen)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? I have got clients crying for this feature

posts.author: Tabish (Tabish)
posts.cookedHtml: Honestly speaking this looks mouthwatering, its quite strange that such a crucial aspect has been overlooked till now

posts.author: LambertStrategy (Avi Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Okay this is getting critical, I don’t know if I can stick with Monday much longer if the Gmail sidebar integration isn’t offered by at least in the next quarter.

I really like Monday in many ways.

But I think it warrants higher importance on Monday’s roadmap. The post and comments are literal feedback on how to make Monday better for existing users/clients/customers.

A Chrome extension or a Gmail Sidebar SDK app is needed STAT- to me, and many others, this would expand the convenient automations, now best used as forwarded emails or Zaps.

More to the point, I found a reason to comment again when the following happened:

I received an email that I wanted to actively add to a board with a date and label of importance, and if I have to set up and configure less in Monday, but that I could do more in Gmail, if there were an approved extension. Also, I would use Monday more often. I am confident that would make me and others happier customers.

posts.author: tarrynjordaan (Tarryn Jordaan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Is there any update on this?

posts.author: angelak (Angela Kwok)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature? I am currently evaluating Monday.com and other tools and the Gmail add-on/extension is an important one for us. We want the ability to add tasks from emails in Gmail selectively, much like Wrike offers in their Gmail add-on.

posts.author: nsimonton (Nick Simonton)
posts.cookedHtml: @melissav-monday.com - any updates? This has been on the list since 2019 and seems to be a pretty common item for most systems like Monday. An update would be appreciated.

posts.author: gcKen (Kenneth Obel)
posts.cookedHtml:
As fairly new Monday users, we have found Monday to be limited/underpowered in how it deals with email and team email sharing.

You can, for example, forward an email to a specific Monday “drop” address and that becomes a task in the related board. However, you first have to go to the project to find/copy the relevant drop address. It’s a bit slow and manual, but maybe that’s all you need?

Yes, an add-in where one could – right from within gmail – specify the project to which to attach an email, would be very useful.

I would like to see even more robust gmail integration that would allow everyone on my team to view emails on projects that they are assigned to, regardless of whether they are actual recipients on the email. Monday has some version of this, it seems, but it would only be able to identify the email by client (not by project), which is not very helpful.

I hope this helps.

KO

posts.author: jiyu (Jimi Yu)
posts.cookedHtml: In addition to adding new item using the sidebar. Can the extension also connect email threads to an existing item? Once connected, any new email to the thread should be logged as an update or something like that.
